description:
You are acting as a deployment engineer who has just rolled out version 1.0.0 of an internal application.  
For compliance you must create a very small SQLite-based audit trail from the Linux command line.

Required end state of the system
1. A directory named “db” must exist at  
   /home/user/deployment/db
2. Inside that directory there must be a SQLite database file named  
   /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db
3. The database must contain exactly one table, named releases, created with the following schema (all columns NOT NULL):
      id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
      version       TEXT  
      deployed_at   TEXT
4. The table releases must contain exactly one row with the following values:
      id          → 1  
      version     → 1.0.0  
      deployed_at → 2022-10-05
5. You must capture the results of the verification query
      SELECT * FROM releases;
   in a log file located at  
   /home/user/deployment/release_check.log
   The *only* content of this log file must be two lines, formatted **exactly** like this (including lower-case column names, vertical-bar separators, and a single trailing newline at the end of the second line):

   id|version|deployed_at  
   1|1.0.0|2022-10-05

Operational constraints
• Accomplish the task exclusively with the standard sqlite3 command-line interface and normal GNU/Linux shell tools already present in a minimal distribution.  
• You should need no more than three SQLite commands in total (an agent might combine several SQL statements in one sqlite3 invocation).  
• Do NOT hard-code absolute paths inside SQL; rely on the working directory or sqlite3’s file argument.  
• Ensure the directory tree /home/user/deployment/db exists before creating the database file, because the agent will not have permission to create new directories elsewhere.  
• When the task is complete, the automated grader will:
  a) Check that /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db is a valid SQLite database.  
  b) Verify that sqlite3 "SELECT * FROM releases;" returns exactly one row with the required values.  
  c) Verify that /home/user/deployment/release_check.log exists and its contents match **exactly** the two-line text block shown above (including the trailing newline).

Deliverables the grader will test
• /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db  
• /home/user/deployment/release_check.log  

No additional files or output are necessary.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

1. Files/directories
   - Directory: /home/user/deployment/db  (regular directory, mode 755 or inherited)
   - File: /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db  (SQLite 3 database)
   - File: /home/user/deployment/release_check.log

2. Database verification
   The command:
      sqlite3 /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db "PRAGMA table_info(releases);"
   must return exactly:
      0|id|INTEGER|1||1
      1|version|TEXT|1||0
      2|deployed_at|TEXT|1||0

   The command:
      sqlite3 /home/user/deployment/db/versioning.db "SELECT * FROM releases;"
   must return exactly:
      1|1.0.0|2022-10-05

3. Log file contents
   Exact byte sequence (including final newline) for /home/user/deployment/release_check.log:

   id|version|deployed_at\n
   1|1.0.0|2022-10-05\n
name: task_000000_f73fa2e8
